data_in.description: SMRT dataset with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular structures from METLIN small molecule dataset

data_out.description: Trained DNN model checkpoint and retention time predictions on test set
Performance metrics: mean absolute error of 39.2±1.2 s and median absolute error of 17.2±0.9 s

domain_knowledge: Cosine annealing with warm restarts (SGDR) cycles the learning rate between high and low values across epochs to escape local minima and improve DNN generalization for regression tasks.
Stochastic weight averaging (SWA) averages model weights across multiple epochs with low constant learning rates to find flatter minima and reduce test error without hyperparameter tuning.

Molecular fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, Path-based) encode chemical substructure patterns as binary vectors and typically outperform high-dimensional descriptor sets for QSAR and retention time prediction.

Heavy regularization (L1/L2 penalties, dropout) is essential when training DNNs on chemical datasets to prevent overfitting on the 80,038-sample SMRT dataset and stabilize predictions across chemical space.

Retention time prediction error is reported as both mean absolute error (robustness across the full distribution) and median absolute error (robustness to outliers); both metrics with ±std reflect cross-validation uncertainty.

inputs: SMRT dataset with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular structures from METLIN small molecule dataset
methodology_summary: Load SMRT dataset (80,038 molecules with experimental retention times from METLIN).
Generate 5,666 descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, Path) using alvaDesc.
Construct feature matrices combining descriptors and fingerprints; split into train/validation/test sets.
Train heavily regularized DNN with cosine annealing warm restarts scheduling and stochastic weight averaging.
Evaluate on test set and compute mean absolute error and median absolute error with uncertainties.
Validation: output MAE = 39.2±1.2 s and MdAE = 17.2±0.9 s matches reported values within reported standard deviations.

title: Reproduce DNN mean and median absolute error on SMRT dataset

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Trained DNN model checkpoint and retention time predictions on test set.
workflow_description: 1. Load SMRT dataset containing 80,038 experimental retention times and corresponding molecular structures from the METLIN small molecule dataset. 2. Generate 5,666 molecular descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, and Path Fingerprints) using alvaDesc software for all molecules. 3. Prepare feature matrices combining descriptors and fingerprints as model inputs and retention times as regression targets. 4. Configure a deeply regularized DNN architecture with heavy regularization (L1/L2 penalties) and train using cosine annealing with warm restarts as the learning rate scheduler. 5. Apply stochastic weight averaging during training to improve generalization. 6. Evaluate trained model on held-out test set and compute mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) with reported uncertainties (±1.2 s and ±0.9 s respectively).
